_README: N-SQUARED (N2) CHART — FILL-IN TEMPLATE
PURPOSE
Capture every subsystem-to-subsystem interface in a single structured file.
Feed this JSON to `n2_from_json.py` to generate an Excel N2 chart with
automatic analysis (system flows, critical subsystems, QFD endpoint check).
HOW TO USE
1. Copy this file: `cp n2-chart-template.json my-project-n2.json`
2. Fill in every section below. Keys prefixed with `_` are comments and
are ignored by the script — leave them or delete them.
3. Run: `python n2_from_json.py my-project-n2.json`
4. Open the generated `<project_id>-n2-chart.xlsx`.
RULES (from the N2 method — see `04 - Formalizing with N-Squared Charts.md`)
- Row = SENDER (provider). Column = RECEIVER. Cell (i,j) = what i provides to j.
- Subsystem names live on the diagonal (auto-placed by the script).
- One JSON file = one interface type. Use `interface_type: operational` for
runtime data flows. Use `interface_type: design` for build-time artifacts
(libraries, contracts, schemas). Do NOT mix the two.
- Keep `name` short (fits in a matrix cell). Put detail in `data`.
- Request/response pairs occupy MIRRORED cells: e.g. (Storefront -> Cart) for
the request and (Cart -> Storefront) for the reply. Define BOTH.

project._instructions: Identifies the chart and the design context it belongs to.
project.name: My Project — N2 Interface Chart
project._qfd_target_endpoints: From your QFD design target. The script compares this to your declared endpoint counts and flags drift > 25%.

subsystems._instructions: List every subsystem ONCE. Order matters — it becomes row/column order in the matrix. 4-9 subsystems is the sweet spot; >10 becomes hard to read.
subsystems.name: Subsystem One
subsystems.description: What this subsystem is responsible for (one sentence).
subsystems._category_options: engine | ui | platform | data | external — used only for color coding

interfaces._instructions: One entry per directional interface. (from, to) must be unique — if two subsystems exchange data both ways, write TWO entries.
interfaces.name: Short Label
interfaces._name: Shows in the matrix cell. Keep under ~25 chars.
interfaces.data: Detailed payload description: fields, units, frequency, trigger.
interfaces._endpoints_count: Approx number of API endpoints / message types this interface covers. Used for QFD reconciliation.

external_interfaces._instructions: Interfaces that cross the system boundary (third-party APIs, browsers, regulators). Listed in a separate table — they don't appear in the N2 matrix itself.

system_flows._instructions: An ordered traversal through the matrix that represents a real user/operational journey. List each subsystem in the order data flows. The script will validate that each consecutive (from, to) pair exists in `interfaces`.
system_flows.name: Checkout Flow
system_flows.description: User browses product, adds to cart, places order.

control_loops._instructions: A system_flow whose path returns to a prior subsystem. The script auto-detects loops; this section lets you NAME and DESCRIBE the ones that matter. The path MUST start and end at the same subsystem and every (from, to) pair must exist in `interfaces`.
control_loops.name: Order ↔ Payment Confirmation Loop
control_loops.description: Order requests payment; payment result returns; order finalizes.
